title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, Delivery Limits, and Parking · Type-System Trace: Quorum Delivery Limit
summary: A queue hardcodes a missing DLX, cycles messages indefinitely, drops quorum poison messages after the default delivery limit, and treats a dead-letter queue as an unowned archive. Planned artifact: Add and verify the quorum delivery limit increment in dead-letter, poison, and remediation topology. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Account for RabbitMQ 4.x quorum delivery limit defaults, x-delivery-count, policy changes, group requeues, and intentional dead-letter retention.
Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Quorum Delivery Limit · Theory Quorum Delivery Limit
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 3174 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to route exhausted poison messages through a bound, owned, and retained remediation path; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Account for RabbitMQ 4.x quorum delivery limit defaults, x-delivery-count, policy changes, group requeues, and intentional dead-letter retention.
